findings.blocker: S6 step (c) HOU on Preadditive sum distribution: `rw [key₂]` / `simp_rw [key₂]` / `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]` / `simp only [Preadditive.sum_comp]` all fail with `pattern (ModuleCat.Hom.hom ((∑ i, ?G i) ≫ ?E)) ?z not found` or `simp made no progress`. Root cause: the summand body `(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ... ((SimplexCategory.δ i) ...) ...` references the outer summation binder `i` at NESTED binding depths, AND there is binder shadowing (inner `Pi.π (fun i ↦ ...)` rebinds the same letter `i`). Lean's HOU pattern matcher cannot determine the `?G` placeholder. The iter-093 `∘ₗ`-unfolding blocker IS RESOLVED (iter-094 breakthrough — see attempt 2), but a NEW HOU site has surfaced at the categorical-level Preadditive distribution.

attempts.insight: Confirms iter-093 dead-end across all reasonable variants. The eqToHom's source ModuleCat is metavariable-driven (from `dif_pos hRel` at L535) so the LinearMap.comp discrimination tree cannot bind. Two NEW variants tested this iter (`dsimp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]`, `rw [show ... from fun ... => rfl]`) also fail.

attempts.strategy: BREAKTHROUGH — `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]`. Instead of unfolding `∘ₗ`-application at LinearMap level, re-fold the entire `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom` into the categorical composition `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom` via the REVERSE direction of Mathlib's `ModuleCat.hom_comp : (f ≫ g).hom = g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom`.
attempts.insight: **Key iter-094 breakthrough**: The reverse-direction rewrite **resolves the iter-093 `∘ₗ`-unfolding blocker entirely** in one line. The LinearMap-level `eqToHom`-source HOU is gone because categorical types are stable under unification (no metavariable-driven source). Trades the iter-093 LinearMap-level blocker for an iter-094 categorical-level distribution problem — a strictly easier surface. Pattern is reusable across the project.

attempts.strategy: Body-local `key₂` with FREE variables to bypass HOU at proof construction time, then `rw [key₂]`.
attempts.code_tried:
have key₂ : ∀ (G : Fin (n + 1) → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k))) (E : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ ModuleCat.of k ((i : Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀) → ↑(Z₂ i))) (z : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k)), ((∑ i, G i) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ i, (G i ≫ E).hom z := by
  intro G E z
  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
  rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum (fun i => G i ≫ E) Finset.univ]
  exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (G i ≫ E).hom) z
rw [key₂]
attempts.lean_error: `have key₂` builds; `rw [key₂]` fails with `pattern `(ModuleCat.Hom.hom ((∑ i, ?G i) ≫ ?E)) ?z` not found`
attempts.goal_after: (unchanged after `rw`; `have key₂` is in scope at L580–L588)
attempts.insight: `have key₂` PROVES cleanly (FREE-variable proof avoids HOU). But `rw [key₂]` still HOU-fails at the call site for the same reason as attempt 3 — the summand's binder-shadowing / nested `i`-references defeat the discrimination tree. **Lesson**: the HOU issue is at pattern-matching, not proof construction. Body-local helpers don't help here. The `key₂` helper is committed at L580–L588 for use by `convert` / `Finset.cons_induction` routes in iter-095.

attempts.insight: Compile-verified delivery of iter-094 sub-step. The breakthrough tactic line at L570 IS the substantive structural advance — it resolves the iter-093 `∘ₗ`-unfolding blocker. The body-local `key₂` helper is the corresponding categorical distributor, ready for `convert` / manual induction routes in iter-095.

next_steps: Iter-095 must resolve the new HOU site on `rw [key₂]` against an `i`-shadowed / multiply-referencing summand. Three concrete routes carried forward from the iter-094 task result: (D′) `convert key₂ G_explicit E_explicit z`-style at the goal, generating equality subgoals dischargeable by `rfl` / `ext` — `convert` bypasses the discrimination tree's strict unification; (D″) `Finset.cons_induction` on `Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (n+1))` to MANUALLY distribute the sum step-by-step (avoiding HOU entirely); (D‴) `Preadditive.comp_sum` (dual) — push `Pi.π Z₂ j` through the categorical sum BEFORE `.hom`-application; the post-`Preadditive.comp_sum` form would be `∑ i, F i ≫ eqToHom ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j`, where the outer `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` is now INSIDE each summand's `≫`. Most robust but requires careful setup. Recommended: try (D′) `convert` first (smallest change to iter-094 commit), fall back to (D″) `Finset.cons_induction` if `convert` doesn't elaborate.
